verified.notes: Age requirement confirmed from official MN DHS source. MSHO is a voluntary managed care program for MA-eligible seniors 65+. Income and asset limits are not program-specific; eligibility is based on Medical Assistance (MA) qualification. Official phone number for MSHO enrollment/assistance not found in provided search results; recommend contacting individual MCO health plans or MN DHS directly.

programs.notes: Age requirement confirmed from official MN DHS source. MSHO is a voluntary managed care program for MA-eligible seniors 65+. Official phone number for MSHO enrollment/assistance not found in provided search results; recommend contacting individual MCO health plans or MN DHS directly.

verified.notes: Income limits shown are for QMB program as of 2026 per LawHelp Minnesota official document (income limits change 7/1/2026). Asset limits are Minnesota-specific and significantly higher than federal minimums. SLMB and QI programs have different income thresholds. Phone number not found in provided search results. Program names and structure remain current as of April 2026.

verified.notes: Multiple regional legal aid programs (e.g., Mid-Minnesota Legal Aid, SMRLS) serve seniors 60+ statewide under MN Board on Aging/Older Americans Act; no universal income/assets test found, prioritized those in greatest need. Statewide intake: 1-877-696-6529.

programs.programName: Minnesota Aging Pathways (formerly Senior LinkAge Line)

verified.notes: Program was rebranded from Senior LinkAge Line to Minnesota Aging Pathways in October 2025. No minimum age requirement, income limits, or asset limits are specified in available official sources. The program is described as serving 'older Minnesotans' and those 'eligible for Medicare benefits' but no specific age threshold is documented. Phone number confirmed from multiple official .gov sources.
